task_completion.description: Agent views the OpenICE Supervisor application to inspect simulated device data and alarm configurations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the OpenICE Supervisor GUI open and visible on the screen, displaying vital signs, waveforms, or alarm settings.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the required alarm audit text file on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) is open with the file saved as '/home/ga/Desktop/alarm_audit.txt', or the file is visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The report includes the specific vital sign parameters examined and their currently configured threshold values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text visible in the report explicitly listing vital signs (e.g., HR, SpO2, RR) alongside their current high/low alarm threshold numbers observed in OpenICE.

task_completion.description: The report assesses whether the current simulated vital sign values fall within or outside normal ICU ranges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text visible in the report comparing the simulated patient values to standard normal ranges (e.g., noting if a simulated HR of 55 is outside the normal 60-100 range).

task_completion.description: The report identifies which alarms are active versus suppressed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text visible in the report explicitly categorizing specific alarms as 'active', 'triggered', 'suppressed', or 'silenced'.

task_completion.description: The report provides specific, evidence-based recommendations for numeric threshold adjustments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text visible in the report suggesting new, specific numeric values for alarm thresholds (e.g., 'adjust HR low alarm to 50 bpm') to reduce false alarms/alarm fatigue.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenICE GUI to gather data rather than hallucinating values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent actively viewing or clicking through the OpenICE Supervisor application windows, not just operating blindly in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent manually drafted the report in a text editor rather than using a single terminal command to echo pre-computed text.
integrity.visual_evidence: A GUI text editor or terminal-based text editor (like nano/vim) is open showing the report being progressively written.

integrity.description: The values reported in the text file match the simulated data visible in the OpenICE application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The simulated vital sign numbers and thresholds written in the text editor match the numbers displayed on the OpenICE Supervisor GUI in earlier or concurrent screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Normal ICU ranges for verification: Heart Rate (HR) is 60-100 bpm, Respiratory Rate (RR) is 12-20 breaths/min, and SpO2 is >= 92%. The required output file must be exactly at /home/ga/Desktop/alarm_audit.txt. The report must contain 4 specific elements: (1) parameters and current thresholds, (2) assessment of simulated values against normal ranges, (3) active vs suppressed alarm states, and (4) specific numeric threshold adjustment recommendations to reduce alarm fatigue.
